heading: National random sample study of child welfare
label: 628b National random sample study of child welfare

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct (directly, or by grant, contract, or interagency agreement) a national study based on random samples of children who are at risk of child abuse or neglect, or are determined by States to have been abused or neglected.
yield data reliable at the State level for as many States as the Secretary determines is feasible.
carefully consider selecting the sample from cases of confirmed abuse or neglect; and

children.label: A
children.text: the type of abuse or neglect involved;

children.label: B
children.text: the frequency of contact with State or local agencies;

children.label: C
children.text: whether the child involved has been separated from the family, and, if so, under what circumstances;

children.label: D
children.text: the number, type, and characteristics of out-of-home placements of the child; and

children.label: E
children.text: the average duration of each placement.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: From time to time, the Secretary shall prepare reports summarizing the results of the study required by subsection (a).

children.heading: Availability
children.label: 2 Availability
children.text: The Secretary shall make available to the public any report prepared under paragraph (1), in writing or in the form of an electronic data tape.

children.heading: Authority to charge fee
children.label: 3 Authority to charge fee
children.text: The Secretary may charge and collect a fee for the furnishing of reports under paragraph (2).

children.heading: Appropriation
children.label: e Appropriation
children.text: Out of any money in the Treasury of the United States not otherwise appropriated, there are appropriated to the Secretary for each of fiscal years 1996 through 2002 $6,000,000 to carry out this section.
